none.justification: Fully matches the GT: identifies object.c checkType/addReplyErrorObject as the construction site, traces both SET-only WRONGTYPE paths via GET and IFEQ/IFNE/IFDEQ/IFDNE, and notes plain SET overwrites without type-checking.

ccodegraph.justification: Fully correct: names src/object.c:887 as the actual reply write, distinguishes shared helper vs SET-specific call sites, covers both t_string.c:119 and SET ... GET via t_string.c:100 -> 467, and explicitly excludes plain SET.

codegraph.justification: Mostly correct on the construction site and the two branches, but its call-chain summary includes setnx/setex/psetex as feeding into the two WRONGTYPE paths without explaining the COMMAND_SET-only option gating, which is a real scope issue called out by the GT.

cbm.justification: Fully correct: pinpoints checkType/addReplyErrorObject as the actual construction, lists both SET-path triggers including the GET subcall and direct IFEQ-family check, and states that plain SET does not WRONGTYPE-check.
